characters.name: Al
characters.description: AlDied1988BackstagePortrayed byAl RosenSeason(s)2,3,4,5,6,7,8First seenFortune and Men's WeightLast seenThe Improbable Dream, Part 2Episodes74Alis a recurring character on NBC seriesCheers. He is portrayed byAl Rosen. Al was a frequently seen barfly through much of the series run. He was usually seen sitting at the back end, usually dressed in a suit or plaid blazer, and almost always with his trademark hat. In at least one episode he's seen with dark rimmed glasses ("Second Time Around"). He spoke with a gravelly old voice. Occasionally he was sitting in a table or booth. Al clearly thought himself the bar stud. In "The Groom Wore Clearasil", Woody takes a phone call for "The Outlaw of Love", and Al assumes it's for him. In "And God Created Woodman", Cliff is selling mail order shoes, and Al orders the "Playboy" model. Al was a frequently seen barfly through much of the series run. He was usually seen sitting at the back end, usually dressed in a suit or plaid blazer, and almost always with his trademark hat. In at least one episode he's seen with dark rimmed glasses ("Second Time Around"). He spoke with a gravelly old voice. Occasionally he was sitting in a table or booth.

characters.name: Boy
characters.description: BoyOccupationnoneBackstagePortrayed byJohn P. Navin Jr.Season(s)1Only seenGive Me a Ring SometimeEpisodes1 ABoywas a one-off character in thefirst episodeofCheers. He attempted to get a drink fromSam Malonewho asked for his ID and he gave a fake military ID that would make him 38, Sam did not believe him and refused to serve him. WhenDiane ChambersandSumner Sloanwalked in talking about their impending marriage he attempted to get a drink from them but he failed.

characters.name: Carla Tortelli
characters.description: Carla TortelliBornBirthdayCarla Maria Victoria Angelina Teresa Apollonia Lozupone1950OccupationWaitress atCheersRelationshipsRomancesEddie LeBec† (husband)Nick Tortelli(ex-husband)FamilyAnthony Tortelli(son)Gino Tortelli(son)Anne Marie(Daughter)Sarafina (daughter)Lucinda (daughter)Ludlow Tortelli (son)Jesse LeBec (son)Elvis LeBec (son)Benito Lozupone (father)Mama Lozupone (mother)Joe (uncle)Sal Lozupone (brother)Angeline Lozupone (Sister)Annette Lozupone(Sister)BackstagePortrayed byRhea PerlmanFirst seenGive Me a Ring SometimeLast seenOne For The RoadCheerful Goodbyes(Frasier)Episodes270 + 1 episode of FrasierCarla Tortelli(nee Lozupone, formerly LeBec) is a principal character on NBC seriesCheers. She is portrayed byRhea Perlman. She is one of only 3 characters that appeared in every episode (The others being Sam and Norm.) She is the Aunt of Frankie. Carla works as a waitress at Cheers. She is also a mother of eight kids. Carla is known for being sarcastic and confrontational to the other characters on the show, often poking fun at them and their problems. She is especially cruel to Cliff Clavin. She is one of few characters who has children. Carla never celebrates Easter (neither does Kelly, Woody, Cliff, Norm, Vera, Rebecca, Frasier, Lillith) .She also doesn't celebrate 4th of July. Carla is perhaps best known for her abrasive personality. Her personality was most frequently revealed in the form of scathing one-liners in which she was seldom part of the original conversation. characters.name: Darrell Stabell
characters.description: "Ah! Why, why? It is still not right!" This article or section isnot "Cheers". For there's no place for a Beacon Bar in the world above. You can helpCheers Wikibyexpanding it. Darrell Stabellplays Norm's boss inAny Friend of Diane's. He is revealed late in the episode to have desired to be a ballet dancer.

characters.name: Dave Richards
characters.description: Dave was a buddy ofSam's. They played baseball together and shared many memories of life on the road. Dave was a personification of Sam's playboy side - Dave had the same slick but transparent pickup lines, and had a showy personality, often dressed in a loud plaid blazer. He had a bumper sticker on his car that read, "Honk if you're horny". Dave was popular with everyone at Cheers saveDianealone. He flirted with her just as he would any attractive woman, but never got anywhere. He was unfazed at the rejections though. Dave was actually married at the start of the series, but he and his wife split by the second season - apparently not amicably, as he revealed that they had "exchanged gunfire".

characters.name: Dennis Hamill
characters.description: Dennis Hamill is the man who made an unsuccessful offer to buySam's corvetteinThe Two Faces of Norm. After unsuccessfully answering a trick question, Sam orders Hamill to leave in a fit of rage.

characters.name: Derek Malone
characters.description: Derek MaloneOccupationLawyerRelationshipsFamilySam MaloneBackstageDerek Maloneis an unseen character on NBC seriesCheers. Derek is the brother of Sam Malone. He is never seen, but he is said to have golden blonde hair, deep blue eyes, and an operatic voice. He can do trick pool shots, and he tap dances. He is an international lawyer and owns his own plane. He makes Sam feel invisible. Derek falls in love with Diane. Derek teaches Spanish to Coach. Derek is a man like any other man you would meet in Greek mythology. Derek is the brother of Sam Malone. characters.name: Don Santry
characters.description: Don isRebecca'sboyfriend duringOne for the Road. Despite proposing to her, Rebecca subconciously replies with "no", causing the plumber to storm out of the bar. However, the plumber sees Rebecca at Melville's withSam andthen proposes again, to which Rebecca screams out an ecstatic yes, and the two quickly elope. By the second season ofFrasier,Santry has made a fortune off a plumbing-related invention and leaves Howe, causing Rebecca to go back to Cheers but as a regular instead of an employee.

characters.name: Doris
characters.description: Doris is Norm's Secretary inThe Two Faces of Norm.

characters.name: Dr. Peter Fisher
characters.description: Peter Fisher, PhD, is a doctor who ran into Sam posing as Dr. Weinstein atThe Café.

characters.name: Eddie Gordon
characters.description: Eddie Gordon was a drinking buddy ofSam's. He shows up unexpectedly at Cheers in the episode "Bar Bet". They haven't seen each other in awhile - presumably due to Sam. Sam doesn't remember Eddie at first, but recalls Eddie when Eddie mentions a bar bet where Sam promised to have married Jacqueline Bisset by a certain date, or he would give the bar to Eddie. Sam laughs off the bet at first, thinking Eddie is kidding with him. But when Eddie produces a piece of paper where Sam wrote out the promise, and indicates he expects to collect the bar, Sam displays his disgust. Eddie says he's obtained a lawyer and is prepared to go to court if necessary, then leaves.

characters.name: Eddie LeBec
characters.description: Eddie LeBecRelationshipsRomancesCarla Tortelli(wife)Gloria (Wife)BackstagePortrayed byJay ThomasSeason(s)5,6and7First seenNever Love a Goalie, Part 1Last seenCall Me IrresponsibleEpisodes9 Gloria (Wife) Guy "Eddie" LeBecis a recurring character onNBC'sCheersand isCarla Tortelli's love interest and later husband. He debuted in thesixteenth episodeof thefifth seasonand was portrayed by the lateJay Thomas. In two-part episodeNever Love a Goalie, Carla meets the Boston Bruins ice hockey player, Eddie LeBec, a French Canadian who is in the middle of a winning streak that he believes is the result from following a strict repeating routine. After a little flirting at Cheers the two begin to date, which unfortunately shortly thereafter brings Eddie's streak to an end. Since both are superstitious, they end their relationship in order to avoid ruining Eddie's ability to play. They reconcile shortly thereafter and then add breaking up to Eddie's routine before every game. InHome Is the Sailor, Carla is revealed to be several months pregnant with Eddie's twins (incorporated by another ofPerlman's pregnancies). In two-part episodeLittle Carla, Happy at Last, after series of confrontations to various superstitions, Carla and Eddie finally wed. InAirport V, Carla almost quits her waitressing job because Eddie said that he would take care of her financially. However, the Bruins released him from his contract due to his age and declining athletic performance, and he could not find another team. Therefore, Eddie ends up as a skating penguin for a travelling ice show in another state. InDeath Takes a Holiday on Ice, Eddie was killed by a Zamboni when he saved the life of another member of the ice show. At the funeral, it was revealed that he had concurrently another wifeGloriawith twins as well. Carla changes her surname back to Tortelli to avoid being confused with the other "Mrs. LeBec".

characters.name: Ernie "Coach" Pantusso
characters.description: Ernie "Coach" PantussoBornBirthdayErnie Pantusso1911Died1985AliasCoachOccupationBartender at CheersRelationshipsRomancesAngela Pantusso(late wife)FamilyLisa Pantusso(daughter)FriendsSam MaloneCarla TortelliBackstagePortrayed byNicholas ColasantoSeason(s)1-3First seenGive Me a Ring SometimeLast seenRescue MeErnie "Coach" Pantussois one of the bartenders at Cheers for the first three seasons, portrayed by the lateNicholasColasanto. Coach was a former baseball player and coach before he eventually started working for Sam at Cheers. He is a bit dimwitted, yet full of life experience. The sweetest guy anybody could ever meet and a helper of all, Coach was a great and powerful character. He is Sam's retired baseball coach. Carla Tortelli Towards the end of the third season, Colasanto became ill and frail and passed away. Since episodes were aired out of order, the remainder of episodes taped that season gave humorous explanations as to where Coach was, until his character was killed off at the beginning of Season 4.  At that time, Coach’s bartending role was stepped in by a similarly naive, pen-pal of his, namedWoody Boyd. In the series, Coach had a wife,Angela, who died before the series premiere, and a daughter,Lisa.

characters.name: Esther Clavin
characters.description: Esther ClavinOccupationUnemployedRelationshipsRomancesCliff Clavin Sr(ex-husband)FamilyCliff Clavin(son)BackstagePortrayed byFrances SternhagenSeason(s)5,6,9,10&11First seenMoney DearestLast seenRebecca Gaines, Rebecca LosesEpisodes7 Esther Clavin, also known as Ma Clavin to her son, is a recurring character onNBC'sCheersand debuts in thethird episodeof thefifth season. She is the mother ofCliff Clavinand is portrayed byFrances Sternhagen. Esther is noted for being a notoriously bad cook as her food is almost completely inedible. She also has Cliff's knack/habit of giving random bits of information and trivia. Though she seems to be far more socially adept and better at getting results than her son. And luckier at love. Esther is noted for being a notoriously bad cook as her food is almost completely inedible. She also has Cliff's knack/habit of giving random bits of information and trivia. Though she seems to be far more socially adept and better at getting results than her son. And luckier at love. After being described toNorm Petersonand other barflies by her son since Season 1, Esther makes her first official appearance in the Season 5 episode "Money Dearest" in which her son plots to have her marry a millionaire in order to inherit his fortune. The plans backfires as Esther hasDuncan Fitzgeralddonate all of his fortune in order to prove that she was not motivated to marry him out of greed. She returns in Season 6 "The Last Angry Mailman" to save the family home from being demolished, but abandons it upon learning how much she will get for selling the property and moves to Florida. Esther makes several return appearances as a recurring character particularly in the later seasons when she returns to Boston to live with her son and again to take control of Cliff's life once again by arranging a wedding, and a party for Cliff and Maggie in seasons 9 and 10 respectively. Esther makes her final appearances in "Look Before you Sleep" of the last season of Cheers and has a big fight with Cliff in front ofSam Malone, who was staying the night and in "Rebecca Gaines, Rebecca Loses" having yet another fight with her middle-aged son and ultimately being placed in a retirement home, but wound up moving back in with him by the end of the episode. Like her son, Esther also has a habit of spouting out useless trivia and also possesses a very domineering and controlling personality, and often relies on guilt tripping her son and others in order to get her way. Esther has been a divorcée since before her initial appearance, but has taken up companionship a few times particularly with a man named Duncan Fitzgerald, and later in Season 10 "Heeeeeere's... Cliffy!" it is implied that she may have began datingJohnny Carsonas well. She may have moved out of Cliff's bachelor pad and gotten herself a place of her own as Sam Malone mentions in the Sixteenth Episode of the Second Season ofFrasier (TV series), that Cliff had read an article on flesh-eating bacteria and

characters.name: Esther Clavin
characters.description: Esther ClavinOccupationUnemployedRelationshipsRomancesCliff Clavin Sr(ex-husband)FamilyCliff Clavin(son)BackstagePortrayed byFrances SternhagenSeason(s)5,6,9,10&11First seenMoney DearestLast seenRebecca Gaines, Rebecca LosesEpisodes7 Esther Clavin, also known as Ma Clavin to her son, is a recurring character onNBC'sCheersand debuts in thethird episodeof thefifth season. She is the mother ofCliff Clavinand is portrayed byFrances Sternhagen. Esther is noted for being a notoriously bad cook as her food is almost completely inedible. She also has Cliff's knack/habit of giving random bits of information and trivia. Though she seems to be far more socially adept and better at getting results than her son. And luckier at love. Esther is noted for being a notoriously bad cook as her food is almost completely inedible. She also has Cliff's knack/habit of giving random bits of information and trivia. Though she seems to be far more socially adept and better at getting results than her son. And luckier at love. After being described toNorm Petersonand other barflies by her son since Season 1, Esther makes her first official appearance in the Season 5 episode "Money Dearest" in which her son plots to have her marry a millionaire in order to inherit his fortune. The plans backfires as Esther hasDuncan Fitzgeralddonate all of his fortune in order to prove that she was not motivated to marry him out of greed. She returns in Season 6 "The Last Angry Mailman" to save the family home from being demolished, but abandons it upon learning how much she will get for selling the property and moves to Florida. Esther makes several return appearances as a recurring character particularly in the later seasons when she returns to Boston to live with her son and again to take control of Cliff's life once again by arranging a wedding, and a party for Cliff and Maggie in seasons 9 and 10 respectively. Esther makes her final appearances in "Look Before you Sleep" of the last season of Cheers and has a big fight with Cliff in front ofSam Malone, who was staying the night and in "Rebecca Gaines, Rebecca Loses" having yet another fight with her middle-aged son and ultimately being placed in a retirement home, but wound up moving back in with him by the end of the episode. Like her son, Esther also has a habit of spouting out useless trivia and also possesses a very domineering and controlling personality, and often relies on guilt tripping her son and others in order to get her way. Esther has been a divorcée since before her initial appearance, but has taken up companionship a few times particularly with a man named Duncan Fitzgerald, and later in Season 10 "Heeeeeere's... Cliffy!" it is implied that she may have began datingJohnny Carsonas well. She may have moved out of Cliff's bachelor pad and gotten herself a place of her own as Sam Malone mentions in the Sixteenth Episode of the Second Season ofFrasier (TV series), that Cliff had read an article on flesh-eating bacteria and

characters.name: Evan Drake
characters.description: Evan Gregory Drake is a recurring character on Cheers' Sixth Season, portrayed byTom Skerritt. Appearing asRebecca Howe's boss when she was first introduced in Season 6, it was revealed she had an unrequited crush on him for the previous two years, so extreme that she stopped dating altogether.  She wanted desperately to impress him enough so he would notice her on a romantic level.  Rebecca became increasingly neurotic around Mr. Drake, and she found herself having to explain herself out of ridiculous and embarrassing situations. Rebecca never got to tell Drake how she felt as he left for a job in Japan with his new loverBackseat Becky, Up Front. She did, however, drive his limousine to the airport and crash into a 7-Eleven when she was blinded with jealousy (she had seen him make out with his girlfriend.)

characters.name: Flynn
characters.description: Flynn is the in-universe mayor ofBoston.

characters.name: Frasier Crane
characters.description: Frasier CraneBornBirthdayFrasier Winslow CraneMarch 1953FromSeattle, WAOccupationPsychiatristRelationshipsRomancesLilith Sternin(ex-wife)Candi(ex-fiancee)Diane Chambers(ex-fiancée)Nanette Guzman(ex-wife)FamilyHester Crane(mother)Martin Crane(father)Frederick Crane(son)Niles Crane (brother)Daphne Moon (sister-in-law)David Crane (nephew)Ronee Lawrence (step-mother)Nikos (cousin)Walt (uncle)Nora (aunt)BackstagePortrayed byKelsey GrammerKendall Schmidt (as a youth)Season(s)3-4 (recurring);5-11 (regular)First seenRebound (Part 1)Last seenOne for the RoadEpisodes203 (+263 episodes ofFrasier+1 episode of Wings)Dr. Frasier Winslow Crane, M.D., Ph.D.,is one of the primary customers who attends Cheers starting in season three, portrayed byKelsey Grammer. Candi(ex-fiancee)Diane Chambers(ex-fiancée)Nanette Guzman(ex-wife) 5-11 (regular) Frasier is a psychiatrist for a living. He is a father toFrederick Crane. Frasier originally started attending Cheers as Sam's psychiatrist and Diane's boyfriend. Originally from Seattle, he was divorced from his first wife,Nanette Guzman, before relocating to Boston.

characters.name: Fred
characters.description: FredRelationshipsFamily12 Siblings (Mostly Formerly)BackstagePortrayed byJohn FiedlerSeason(s)1, 5First seenThe Tortelli TortLast seenRelief BartenderEpisodes2 Fred, also known as Freddie is a minor character onCheers. He is abarregular who celebrates every time one of him siblings dies because it leads to him getting more money. The first few celebrations to die were considered by the people of the bar to be shallow but after awhile they celebrated with him, by his first appearance he had lost more than eight of his siblings (the latest being his sister Louise) with at least his brother Bob left alive. InThe Tortelli Torthe arrived at the bar to announce the death of Louise to the celebration of the bar though it confusedDiane. He soon left and was looking forward to the death of his brother Bob.

characters.name: Frederick Crane
characters.description: Frederick CraneBornBirthdayFrederick Gaylord CraneOctober 1989AliasFreddieFromBoston, MARelationshipsFamilyLilith Sternin(mother)Frasier Crane(father)BackstagePortrayed byChristopher Graves, Kevin GravesFrederick Craneis the sole child ofFrasier CraneandLilith Sternin. He is still a young child when the series ends, but it is revealed inFrasierthat he is Jewish, and lives with his mother in Boston after the divorce. Like his parents, he is an intellectual - managing to win the National Spelling Bee. He makes several appearances in Cheers as a baby and a toddler, but has grown up a bit by his firstFrasier (TV series)appearances. On Cheers he is portrayed by twin actorsChristopher and Kevin Gravesand by Luke Tarsitano andTrevor Einhornon Frasier (original show) and by Jack-Cutmore Scott on the 2023 revival show. He is still a young child when the series ends, but it is revealed inFrasierthat he is Jewish, and lives with his mother in Boston after the divorce. Like his parents, he is an intellectual - managing to win the National Spelling Bee. He makes several appearances in Cheers as a baby and a toddler, but has grown up a bit by his firstFrasier (TV series)appearances. On Cheers he is portrayed by twin actorsChristopher and Kevin Gravesand by Luke Tarsitano andTrevor Einhornon Frasier (original show) and by Jack-Cutmore Scott on the 2023 revival show. By the revival show, Frederick (now going by the nickname Freddy) had gotten accepted to Harvard, but ended up dropping out and becoming a firefighter.   Trivia · In Cheers, his first word was Norm.

characters.name: Gary
characters.description: Garywas the owner ofGary's Olde Towne Tavern, which frequently competed in games of sport or pride against Cheers, either for trophy, bragging rights or an act of humiliation. After the rivalry was established in season 4, beginning in season 6 one episode per year, all but once called "Bar Wars" , featured a contest between the two pubs. The Cheers gang almost invariably lost to Gary, with the exception of season 4's bowling competition, season 7's 'Bloody Mary' contest, season 9's basketball competition, and season 11's Bar Wars finale, in whichHarry "The Hat" Gittestricks Gary into demolishing his own pub for a worthless check. Gary was played alternately by both Joel Polis and Robert Desiderio.

characters.name: Gus
characters.description: "Ah! Why, why? It is still not right!" This article or section isnot "Cheers". For there's no place for a Beacon Bar in the world above. You can helpCheers Wikibyexpanding it. Not to be confused with another prior owner of Cheers,Gus O'Malley.

characters.name: Gus O'Malley
characters.description: Gus O'MalleyOccupationRetiredformerlyOwner ofCheersBackstagePortrayed byPat HingleOnly seenThe Last Picture ShowGus O'Malleywas the owner of Cheers beforeSam Malone. It is said that he sold the bar so he could live the last few years of his life in adventure (O'Malley had a heart condition). He only appeared in "Last Picture Show", in which he takes charge while Sam leaves for the night. However, during this, he proves himself to be a very strict boss, much to the dismay ofCarlaandRebecca. Despite his attitude, O'Malley proves himself to be the drastically more profitable than Sam's administration. Continuity: In Season 1 Episode 2 "Sam's Women",Coachtells a patron who comes looking for Gus that Gus is dead. In the same episodeNormsays that Gus was the owner 2 owners back. It is said that he sold the bar so he could live the last few years of his life in adventure (O'Malley had a heart condition). He only appeared in "Last Picture Show", in which he takes charge while Sam leaves for the night. However, during this, he proves himself to be a very strict boss, much to the dismay ofCarlaandRebecca. characters.name: Harry "The Hat" Gittes
characters.description: Harry "The Hat" GittesOccupationcon-artist, card-shark, fast-talker, jail-birdBackstagePortrayed byHarry AndersonFirst seenSam at ElevenLast seenBar Wars VII:  The Naked PreyEpisodes6 (Seasons 1-2, 6, 11)Harry Gittesis a recurring character onCheers. He is played by the late Harry Anderson. At 6'4", thin, and lanky during the first two seasons, Harry was well dressed in a suit and suspenders, glasses, and known for his trademark fedora hat. Harry should not be confused with any of Cheers's regularBarflies, although well-known at Cheers, he was never entirely welcome. Harry often attempts to swindle its customers and employees with various short cons, usually trying to make a quick buck or bum a free drink, but occasionally he lifted wallets or watches. Sam has standing orders to have Harry thrown out of the bar on sight, yet Harry has helped the gang at Cheers on occasion, partly out of sentiment, and partly to protect the Cheers bunch from operators even more pernicious than he. As Harry notes, "I don't like the idea of someone else plucking my pigeons." At 6'4", thin, and lanky during the first two seasons, Harry was well dressed in a suit and suspenders, glasses, and known for his trademark fedora hat. Harry should not be confused with any of Cheers's regularBarflies, although well-known at Cheers, he was never entirely welcome. Harry often attempts to swindle its customers and employees with various short cons, usually trying to make a quick buck or bum a free drink, but occasionally he lifted wallets or watches. Sam has standing orders to have Harry thrown out of the bar on sight, yet Harry has helped the gang at Cheers on occasion, partly out of sentiment, and partly to protect the Cheers bunch from operators even more pernicious than he. As Harry notes, "I don't like the idea of someone else plucking my pigeons." Harry appeared four times in the series’ first and second seasons, In "Pick a Con... Any Con", Sam bails Harry out of jail, in exchange for Harry's help whenCoachis hustled out of $8000. After which he disappeared until season six'sA Kiss Is Still a Kiss. In his season 6, cold-opening cameo, Harry casually explained his long absence "two to ten, with time off for good behavior". The real reason was that actor-magician Harry Anderson had achieved fame in his own right by starring in his own seriesNight Court, which followedCheerson NBC's Thursday nightMust See TVline up. In "Bar Wars VII: The Naked Prey", Harry helps Cheers get their final 'revenge' onGary's Old Town Tavern.
